items.headline: New electrical infrastructure permitted for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: Palo Alto has issued a permit for significant electrical upgrades to 3210 Porter Drive, including new transformers and a substation connection to support expanded research and development operations. The project reflects continued demand for tech and R&D space in the city's commercial areas.

items.headline: Four townhomes planned behind historic house on Crescent Avenue
items.summary: A development project at 434 Crescent Avenue would add four three-story townhomes in the rear while preserving the existing 960-square-foot historic house. The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing the resource alteration permit to ensure the project respects the property's historic character.

items.headline: City Council approves new digital signage agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: San José amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects where and how digital ads will appear throughout the city.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward with council action
items.summary: The City Council approved actions related to replacing elevators at a terminal facility. Details on location, cost, and timeline would indicate whether this affects major transit or civic infrastructure residents use.

items.headline: City seeks bids for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: San José issued a Request for Proposals for encampment cleanup services, suggesting the city is formalizing how it addresses homeless encampments on public property.

items.headline: City negotiating purchase of Finch Avenue property with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to acquire the property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with price and payment terms still being worked out. This could signal a potential development or public use project, though details remain confidential during negotiations.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a comprehensive parks and trails plan for the Innovation District area, which will guide development of new green spaces and pedestrian/bike infrastructure in this growing part of Milpitas. This plan shapes how the district evolves over the coming years in terms of public amenities and connectivity.

items.headline: Two major apartment complexes permitted at El Paseo De Saratoga
items.summary: Building permits issued for 772 new apartments across two buildings at El Paseo De Saratoga, representing a $125.7 million residential development. The twin projects significantly expand housing supply in San José's Saratoga area.
